learning_outcomes:
CHESB 1. Major
Programme: Knowledge
outcomes: • A broad-based
and integrated comprehension of the complexities of Chinese history, culture,
languages and contemporary society, including the integrated realms of the
economy, governance, and business. • A high-level
of knowledge acquired through the direct social and practical engagement with
Chinese society through social-cultural “immersion” and “on-the-ground”
experiential and social enterprise learning. • A focused
area of specific topical knowledge about China explored through intensive
empirical research (mainly the capstone research sequence in the final year). • An
understanding and appreciation of the existing and developing multiple needs
and opportunities for the shared productive action of Chinese citizens and
members of the international community together with respect to global
challenges. Skills
outcomes: • A high level
of proficiency in Chinese language (Putonghua), including in reading and
writing Chinese, at the level needed to be China-specialist analysts,
effective cross-cultural communicators and social network builders linking
China with the world. • A high level
of spoken and written English language ability to support effective communication
in cross-cultural discussions about China in international arenas. • A high level
ability in critical thinking, rational analysis, logical persuasive
argumentation, and original empirical research necessary for today’s China
specialist analysts. • A notable
skill level at effective cross-cultural problem solving, negotiation, and
joint enterprise development gained through the social-cultural “immersion”
experience and “on-the-ground” in China experiential and social enterprise
learning. Values
and attitude outcomes: • A high level
of cross-cultural respect, tolerance, appreciation and compassion, along with
an attentiveness to reflection on essential social-ethical values, drawn from
interactive student discussions, social-cultural immersion, experiential and
social enterprise learning in China.

Major Programme Requirement
Students are required to complete a
minimum of 72 units of

Total:  |  72
Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  CHES
courses at 2000 and above level as included in
the Major programme requirements will be included
in the calculation of Major GPA for honours classification.
2.  |  Students are assigned to one of the streams based on
language assessment upon admission, subject to the approval of the
Programme Director.
3.  |  Any
CHES courses counted towards the fulfillment of one of the above listed
requirements cannot be counted towards the fulfillment of any other
requirements specified in the study scheme.
4.  |  All
CLCP courses taken to fulfill this Major programme requirement cannot be
counted towards the fulfillment of the University Core requirements.
[a]  |  Subject
to the approval of the Programme Director, students fluent in Putonghua
can fulfill this requirement by taking Cantonese (CLCC) courses.
[b]  |  Students
plan to enroll in CHES3401, 3402, 4003, 4004, 4005 must submit
applications for approval by a faculty member and the Programme Director.
[ ]  |  Old course code used in 2024-25 and
before.
